current_claim: On one frozen natural-product-search population, Qwen3, TEM, and InstructRec respond to history and obtain significant target-repeat gains, while none establishes same-checkpoint history increment on the larger target-nonrepeat/no-candidate-overlap surface.
decision: Use this bounded recurrence-transfer separation as Motivation V1. Qwen is the binding frozen confirmation; TEM and InstructRec are same-population one-checkpoint extensions.
supported: All three selected rankers pass the target-repeat positive control.
All three have a positive repeat-minus-no-overlap contrast with a query-cluster bootstrap interval above zero.
None establishes reliable no-overlap nonrepeat history increment.
Near-zero TEM and InstructRec aggregate gains are surface cancellation rather than score invariance.
not_supported: Every Transformer or LLM has this behavior.
Every model receives positive aggregate history gain.
Motivation V1 passes a proposed-system Failure Card.
remaining_controls: Frozen multi-seed TEM and InstructRec confirmation for a family-prevalence claim.
Preferably replication on a second natural-search population.
